QQQ.data_anomaly: QQQ price $711.23 is outside expected range ($250-$700). Verify data source — may be contract rollover, unit mismatch, or genuine historic move.

macro_summary: US equities rallied on Friday as the April jobs beat fueled record highs for SPY (+0.85%) and QQQ (+2.37%), while SOXX surged 5.5%. Oil slipped to $99.74 on fading Hormuz fears, but Trump's rejection of an Iranian peace proposal over the weekend threatens renewed volatility. Upcoming CPI (May 12) and PPI (May 13) serve as the next macro catalysts.
commodities_note: WTI held at $99.74, down 6.3% for the week as ceasefire talks eased supply fears, though weekend developments have pushed futures back above $100. Gold dipped 0.6% to $4,657; DXY flat at 98.15. US10Y ticked down to 4.36%. VIX remains subdued at 17.19, but the Iran risk may spike if peace negotiations stall.

singapore.summary: Singapore banks continue to grind higher despite unresolved NIM compression, with DBS and OCBC now hard-blocked on extension and overbought RSI. UOB is the only non-warning name, but volume is low and the setup lacks urgency. The dual headwind of elevated US10Y and MAS S$NEER appreciation persists, with no relief until at least October 2026.
singapore.key_development: DBS and OCBC surged to hard-block territory; OCBC's 3.7% jump Friday pushed it 6.6% above the 50-day with RSI 72.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX vaulted 5.5% to 520.30, fueled by an AI memory supercycle and relentless hyperscaler capex, but eleven of eleven members are now on CAUTION, most with extreme extension. The rally is broad-based but not buyable — every name is priced for perfection with skewed risk/reward. The few stocks with better R:R (TSEM, CRWV) face immediate earnings binaries.
semiconductors.key_development: AMD, INTC, MU, SK Hynix and others are 60-100% above their 50-day averages; entry requires a 30-50% pullback to restore workable risk/reward.

big_tech.summary: AMZN and GOOG are extreme extension watch; MSFT is the lone name near its 50-day but with a weak R:R. PLTR, despite a pullback, still trades at 67x forward earnings with a distorted setup. The cluster lacks entry points entirely this week.
big_tech.key_development: GOOG at 23% above 50-day, AMZN 18% extended — both need double-digit pullbacks to become interesting on a cash basis.

interconnected.name: Coherent Corp.
interconnected.reason: Received a matching $2B investment from NVDA alongside LITE; both are building US-based photonics manufacturing for AI datacenter interconnects.
interconnected.entry_note: Add to watchlist if COHR pulls back 15-20% from current levels, or after LITE's next earnings confirms AI optics revenue acceleration.

interconnected.name: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co.
interconnected.reason: TSMC is the sole foundry for NVDA, AMD, and AVGO's custom ASICs; its CoWoS packaging expansion directly enables the AI capacity buildout.
interconnected.entry_note: Already on watchlist as TSM, so no additional entry needed.

interconnected.name: Broadcom Inc.
interconnected.reason: Already on watchlist; its custom ASIC business (Google TPU, Meta) is a direct beneficiary of hyperscaler capex.
interconnected.entry_note: Already on watchlist as AVGO, so no additional entry needed.

active_risks: US-Iran Hormuz conflict: exchange of fire and stalled peace talks keep oil elevated and supply chains disrupted.
US-China trade war: Section 122 surcharges and China's rare earth export controls threaten semiconductor supply chains.
US export controls on AI chips and semiconductor design software target Chinese firms, risking retaliation and supply fragmentation.
MATCH Act proposal would restrict ASML's DUV/EUV exports to China, adding legislative uncertainty.
geopolitical.market_impact: Oil hovering at $100 pressures Singapore banks via NIM compression and keeps BigTech margins under scrutiny. Semis cluster caught between structural AI demand and extreme extension — the geopolitical risks are priced into stretched valuations, leaving no margin for error.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: Trump rejected an Iranian peace proposal over the weekend, per Yahoo Finance; oil futures jumped back above $100.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: Remain on cash; no new entries until the extreme extension across semis resets or a macro catalyst (CPI, peace deal) creates a pullback with improved R:R.

portfolio_snapshot.risk_posture: Eighteen of 21 names are on CAUTION, the vast majority from extreme extension hard blocks. The market is pricing perfection across semis and BigTech; any negative surprise (CPI, Hormuz flare-up, earnings miss) could trigger rapid mean reversion with no safety net.

watch_for_entry.note: 4.7:1 R:R with support at $7.34; May 13 earnings is the binary. Wait for print to clear — no entry pre-earnings. Fragility: ADR/China delisting risk, starter size only.
watch_for_entry.entry_level: After May 13 earnings, enter on pullback to $7.23-7.47 with stop below $7.23

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: Core CPI print below 3.0% would ease rate-hike fears and potentially lower US10Y below 4.20%, relieving NIM compression for Singapore banks.
bullish_upgrades: D05_SI → remains blocked until US10Y <4.20%, no upgrade
NVDA → HOLD candidate if long-end yields decline
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Hot CPI above 3.5% would push US10Y higher, strengthen DXY, and deepen extension blocks across semis as rate-sensitive growth re-rates lower.
bearish_impact: Singapore banks → deeper CAUTION (NIM headwind intensifies)
Tech CAUTION → remains blocked; no relief
AMZN, GOOG, AVGO → stay extremely extended

macro_trigger_map.bullish_outcome: A formal ceasefire and reopening of the Strait would crash WTI back toward $80, easing inflation fears and potentially sparking a broad risk-on rally.
bullish_upgrades: O39_SI → could upgrade to HOLD or even WATCH if NIM relief materializes
PLTR → defense premium fades, may drop further toward support
macro_trigger_map.bearish_outcome: Stalled talks and fresh escalation keep oil above $100, prolonging the caution regime and hurting consumer-exposed names.
bearish_impact: Singapore banks → structural NIM compression deepens
BE → re-rated lower as energy uncertainty spikes
